title: Area of trapezoids
description: Practice finding the areas of trapezoids.
topic_path: Module 5: Area, surface area, and volume problems
Topic A: Area of triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the area.**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 3]] square meters
hints: This figure is a quadrilateral with a pair of parallel sides (the top and bottom sides), so it's a trapezoid.
$\text{Area of a trapezoid} = \dfrac12 \cdot (b_1 + b_2) \cdot h$

Now use this formula to calculate the trapezoid's area.

 $b_1 = 7$

 $b_2 = 8$

 $h = 4$

 $A = \dfrac12 \cdot (7 + 8) \cdot 4 = 30$

options.alt: A 4-sided figure with one pair of parallel sides. One parallel side measures 7 meters and the other measures 8 meters. A 4 meter segment connects the parallel sides and is at a right angle to the 7-meter side.
